comments.body: reran `vndr`. some lines are updated by `vndr`

@AkihiroSuda after you edited `vndr.conf` you should have just ran:

```
vndr github.com/containers/image
vndr github.com/BurntSushi/toml
```

Please do that and discard any other fixes/changes you did, let's make a separate PR for that :heart:

> @AkihiroSuda after you edited `vndr.conf` you should have just ran:
> 
> ```
> vndr github.com/containers/image
> vndr github.com/BurntSushi/toml
> ```
> 
> Please do that and discard any other fixes/changes you did, let's make a separate PR for that :heart: 

That is locally convenient, but it also makes it way too easy for `vendor.conf` and the contents of `vendor` to drift away.

I guess both local vendor updates like the above (_if_ the complete refresh happens frequently enough), and a full `vndr` re-run within an otherwise unrelated PR, are fine with me.

(<s>Non-ABI-stable dependencies and vendoring and staying on a specific commit without future bugfixes is all problematic, \*grumble\*</s> I don’t have a good solution given the existing ecosystem, \*shuts up\*.)

---

The current test failure looks like a missing `git add`.

revendored

- I needed to use older vndr (https://github.com/LK4D4/vndr/tree/78865401d6d5c6ec64f47cbdf0b5dd1c8b42c331, because the current vndr requires me to rewrite `vendor.conf` to the new format
- Eliminated vendoring for toml (unneeded for this case)

This updates `vendor.conf`to point to a newer containers/storage version, yet the PR is not actually updating containers/storage, and the dependency necessary for that new version is not listed either; that’s just a trap left for the next person to run `vndr`.

Please, either run `vndr` only `containers/image`, in which case leave the other repos in `vendor.conf` unmodified, or do a full `vndr` run where both the `vendor` directory and `vendor.conf` are fully consistent.
github says "Pull request successfully merged and closed" but it does not seems merged..

and hence rebase & push -f resulted in closing this PR
